Package org.eventb.ui.prover

Interface IProofCommand

All Known Implementing Classes:
Backtrack, Info, Next, NextPending, NextReview, Previous, Prune, Review, SearchHypotheses, ShowCache
public interface IProofCommand
Since:
1.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    apply(IUserSupport us, Predicate hyp, String[] inputs, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor)
    Apply the proof command.
    boolean
    isApplicable(IUserSupport us, Predicate hyp, String input)
    Indicates if the proof command is applicable at the current state or not.

  • Method Details

    • isApplicable

      boolean isApplicable(IUserSupport us, Predicate hyp, String input)
      Indicates if the proof command is applicable at the current state or not.

      Parameters:
      us - the User Support, see IUserSupport.
      hyp - the current hypothesis or null if the this is checking for goal.
      input - the string input to the proof command. It is the input taken from the input area in the Proof Control View, or null otherwise.
      Returns:
      true if the proof command is applicable, otherwise, return false.

    • apply

      void apply(IUserSupport us, Predicate hyp, String[] inputs, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Apply the proof command.

      Parameters:
      us - the User Support, see IUserSupport.
      hyp - the current hypothesis or null if the this is apply to goal.
      inputs - the inputs of the tactic which are an array of strings, there are several possibilities for different types of tactics
      • Global tactic: The input is an array contains a single string taken from the input area in the Proof Control View.
      • Goal/Hypothesis tactics: This inputs only applied to instantiate quantified predicates. This is the list of string used to instantiate taken from the Proving User Interface.
      monitor - a progress monitor.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - when there is a problem applying the command.
